scope.summary: Constitution rule 13 plus `swarm_discipline.sh foreign-edit`: a worktree file that differs from HEAD is reported as another agent mid-edit, so a mid-edit buffer can no longer be read as a build blocker.
out_of_scope: The four earlier incidents in the same session, carried by the sibling beads under oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.
The rust-oracledb half: its AGENTS.md still carries the twelve-rule constitution and none of these mechanisms.
Ownership itself: git cannot say which agent owns a dirty path, so the check answers 'does this differ from HEAD' and points at the committed truth rather than naming a pane.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh foreign-edit
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/swarm_discipline.sh --selftest` asserts both directions in a throwaway repo: an unmodified path reports AT-HEAD and exits 0; the same path after an edit exits 65. Run against this live shared checkout it printed FOREIGN-EDIT for crates/oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs [MM] and AT-HEAD for crates/oraclemcp-guard/src/lib.rs, with the `git show HEAD:<path>` next step.

limitations: Advisory by construction: the check refuses a verdict, it cannot stop an agent that never runs it. CI runs the selftest, not the per-path query.
